title: Dashboard with table widget - choose which columns to show
excerpt:
Hi Monday folks 
Using a dashboard with 47 connected boards 
Using the Table WIdget 
For the Choose which columns to show 
Do i really have to go through each board one by tedious one to select the 7 columns that i want …

posts.author: iruser (iruser)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday folks

Using a dashboard with 47 connected boards

Using the Table WIdget

For the Choose which columns to show

Do i really have to go through each board one by tedious one to select the 7 columns that i want on the dashboard view?

image356×566 16.6 KB

image340×438 14.3 KB

I have to uncheck all columns for this board and then recheck the seven columns, and i have do that 47 times?

Is there a better / faster / more efficient way?

thanks!

posts.author: trevor13 (Trevor Chauvin-DeCaro)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!

Unfortunately, I dont think there isn’t currently a bulk or global column selection option for the Table Widget when you’re working with multiple connected boards. At the moment:

  1. You do need to configure the visible columns for each board individually, even if all boards share the same column structure.
  2. This can definitely feel tedious with 47 boards, especially when you’re showing only a few columns.

Workarounds

  1. Duplicate Configured Widgets:
  • Configure the Table Widget for one board with the desired columns.
  • Then duplicate that widget and switch the source board(s).
  • You’ll still need to tweak the setup, but it’s faster than starting from scratch each time.
  1. Use Board Mirroring or Roll-Up Boards:
  • Instead of connecting 47 boards directly, consider creating a master roll-up board that mirrors the key items from all 47 boards.
  • Connect the dashboard to the master board, reducing the number of times you configure columns.

posts.author: iruser (iruser)
posts.cookedHtml:
thanks for the suggestions. Would the duplicate widget would result in 47 separate dashboards? is roll up board an add on from marketplace or are you using phrasing that matches market place for an out of box feature?

ian

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there!

Unfortunately, there’s no built-in “apply to all boards” switch in the Table Widget UI. You really do have to pick your seven columns per board if you’re only using the out-of-the-box config screen. But you’ve got a few ways to shortcut the pain:

1. Duplicate and Switch Trick

  • Configure one widget on Board 1 with your seven columns
  • Duplicate that widget (hover → … → Duplicate)
  • In the copy, switch out the source board
  • You’ll still confirm the columns, but you start from your “good” list instead of all unchecked

2. Roll-Up (Master) Board

  • Create a single “Master” board that mirrors just the key items from all 47 boards
  • Mirror the same seven columns into that Master board
  • Point your dashboard’s Table Widget at the Master board
  • Now you set the seven columns exactly once—and everything upstream still flows in

Feature Request
If neither feels quite right, head over to monday.com’s Feature Requests board and ask for a “global column template” or bulk-apply option for Table Widgets. The more of us who upvote it, the sooner it’ll make it onto their roadmap

Good luck, and may your clicks be few!
